title: question: tool for adding insecure registries
body:
What is the best way to add new insecure registries without the need to manually update the file /etc/containers/registries.conf

I could not google it out.

comments.body:
Can you make use of:

--registries.d dir use registry configuration files in dir (e.g. for container signature storage), overriding the default path.

and use a different file or are you wanting to specify the insecure registries on the command line itself?

I’m not aware of any software package for editing the registries configuration; there are readers in Golang (`github.com/containers/image/pkg/sysregistries{,v2}`) and Python (https://github.com/projectatomic/registries ), but neither can update the file.

Of course something I don’t know about may exist.
The intent has always been to manually edit the file; theoretically something could be created.  Is this being done via automation where manually editing is not feasible ?

> The intent has always been to manually edit the file; theoretically something could be created.

IMHO, the intent in the world of Ansible, Chef, Salt and Puppet was not a good decision.

>  Is this being done via automation where manually editing is not feasible ?

Exactly! I wanted to use sed but the format is so `*` that I would have to use a complicate awk command to achieve my goal.

> --registries.d dir use registry configuration files in dir (e.g. for container signature storage), overriding the default path

Thank you for the suggestion. Unfortunately, I am not sure which command I am supposed to use with the parameter.

I need to start docker service and I need to pull docker images from our internal docker registries. Nothing special - just a VM. But I refuse to configure the VM manually.

What about to define an augeas lens?
http://augeas.net/index.html
@jfilak i could write up a fairly simple tool for you ... interested in something like that ?
No need to create a tool just for me. I can live with an awk command ;) If you have better things to do, ignore this issue. I will close it when I have the awk command tested.
Once written, and with your permission, it would probably be handy to drop the tool somewhere in here if you think others could use it.

Here you are:

```bash
awk -v INS_REG="blah" -i inplace -v INPLACE_SUFFIX=.bak 'BEGIN { ins=0; out=1 } /^\[reg.*.ins.*\]$/ { ins=1 } match($0, /^(reg.*= *\[)(\].*)$/, mch) { if (ins==1) { printf "%s'\''%s'\''%s\n", mch[1], INS_REG, mch[2]; ins=0; out=0; } } { if (out==1) print $0; out=1 }' /etc/containers/registries.conf
```

Just replace the value of INS_REG with your insecure registry host name.
Tested with https://github.com/projectatomic/registries/blob/master/registries.conf

It is disgusting and it works only for 'registries = []'. I opened this issue in the hope there is a better way. Perhaps, I can create a PR for https://github.com/projectatomic/registries (I opened the issue here, because `rpm -qf /etc/containers/registries.conf` returned skopeo).

BTW: this can be the solution: https://github.com/hercules-team/augeas/pull/91
